creator: bz@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Justin Hibbits from comment #15)

I also saw it with rgephy after about 50-60 days on a different board.

So with my changed driver I sometimes see:

[3300001.688880] dpaa2_dpmac3: sc->link_state 2 == link_state 2. Forcing update.
[3300001.688926] dpaa2_dpmac3: sc->link_state 2 == link_state 2. Forcing update.
[3300002.735026] dpaa2_dpmac3: sc->link_state 2 == link_state 2. Forcing update.

which, I think, means we get an interrupt for it but there was no change.
But that's not this issue.


Then I found this in the logs:

[4524430.543893] dpni3: link state changed to DOWN
[4524430.546210] dpni3: 2 link states coalesced
[4524430.546215] dpni3: link state changed to DOWN
[4524430.553694] dpni3: link state changed to UP
[4549124.900410] dpni3: promiscuous mode enabled
[4549138.670429] dpni3: promiscuous mode disabled

I assume the promisc was when I started to debug it 6-7 hours later:


dev.dpaa2_ni.3 stats did not show much but the extra sysctl I have here said (note the DOWN):

# sysctl dev.dpaa2_ni.3.link
dev.dpaa2_ni.3.link:
Link State: DOWN (IGNORE)
Link Rate: 0
Link Options: 0
Link Speed Capabilities: 000000000000000000
Link Speed Advertised for autoneg: 000000000000000000

So "ni" thought the link was down.


ifconfig obviously did not think like that:

# ifconfig -m dpni3
dpni3: flags=1008843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 1500
        options=8002b<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,VLAN_MTU,JUMBO_MTU,LINKSTATE>
        capabilities=8002b<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,VLAN_MTU,JUMBO_MTU,LINKSTATE>
        ether ....
        media: Ethernet autoselect (1000baseT <full-duplex>)
        status: active
        supported media:
                media autoselect
                media 1000baseT mediaopt full-duplex,master
                media 1000baseT mediaopt full-duplex
                media 100baseTX mediaopt full-duplex
                media 100baseTX
                media 10baseT/UTP mediaopt full-duplex
                media 10baseT/UTP
                media none
        nd6 options=21<PERFORMNUD,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>


Trying to fix it again:

# ifconfig dpni3 media autoselect
dpaa2_dpmac3: sc->link_state 1 == link_state 1. Forcing update.

And after that:

# sysctl dev.dpaa2_ni.3.link
dev.dpaa2_ni.3.link:
Link State: UP (IGNORE)
Link Rate: 0
Link Options: 0
Link Speed Capabilities: 000000000000000000
Link Speed Advertised for autoneg: 000000000000000000

And "ni" thinks the link is UP again and all started working again.

This is what was in the log:

[4558309.018357] dpaa2_dpmac3: sc->link_state 1 == link_state 1. Forcing update.
[4558309.026876] dpni3: link state changed to DOWN
[4558313.000454] dpni3: link state changed to UP

For reference the "link_state" numbers (2 above and the 1 down here) come from sys/net if I remember the code correctly:
#define LINK_STATE_UNKNOWN      0       /* link invalid/unknown */
#define LINK_STATE_DOWN         1       /* link is down */
#define LINK_STATE_UP           2       /* link is up */


So when this all happened the internal link_state was indeed DOWN and not UP though the last "link state changed" message logged was to "UP" before.

I am still confused a bit and will try to add a bootverbose log for all internal "link_state" changes to see.
I'll also try again (as I find time) to push some of these changes into review so you guys can use them for debugging as well.


Hope this helps to start solving the mystery.
